cached_entries.title: Foundry Branching renamed to Global Branching
cached_entries.description: Foundry Branching has been renamed to Global Branching across the platform. You will see this new name reflected in both the Global Branching application and other applications that interact with branches. All functionality remains the same.

cached_entries.title: Manual object selection for code function cards in Quiver
cached_entries.description: **Code function object set** and **Code function categorical plot** cards in Quiver now support manually selecting an object for functions with an object parameter. Previously, these parameter values could only be provided by referencing another card, but now you can select objects directly within the card editor.

cached_entries.title: Support for faster compute for improved pipeline performance
cached_entries.description: HyperAuto pipelines now support faster compute backed by DataFusion, providing up to 12x speed improvements over standard Spark compute. You can create new pipelines with faster compute enabled or switch existing batch pipelines to this compute type through the **Batch Compute Setting**s section of the pipeline configuration.

cached_entries.title: Iceberg supports Google Cloud Storage for bring-your-own-bucket storage
cached_entries.description: Foundry Iceberg now supports Google Cloud Storage (GCS) as a bring-your-own-bucket (BYOB) storage option. You can now store Foundry Iceberg tables in customer-owned GCS buckets, in addition to the existing support for AWS S3 and Azure ADLS. Learn more about [Iceberg architecture options](/foundry/iceberg/managed-virtual/) and [setting up BYOB Iceberg storage](/foundry/iceberg/iceberg-byob/).

cached_entries.title: Improved visibility for sampling strategy errors
cached_entries.description: Input sampling strategy errors now appear as a node badge on the graph and in the **Pipeline errors** panel.

cached_entries.title: Preserve color groups when copying and pasting nodes
cached_entries.description: Pipeline Builder now preserves color group assignments when you copy and paste nodes using keyboard shortcuts (Ctrl+C / Ctrl+V). Previously, color groups were maintained when duplicating nodes locally (Ctrl+D), but were lost when using clipboard operations. This improvement ensures consistent color preservation across all duplication methods, including when pasting nodes between different pipelines.

cached_entries.title: Function and action monitors now support Workflow Lineage as a dynamic scope
cached_entries.description: Function and action monitors now support Workflow Lineage as a new dynamic scope. You can set up a monitor that automatically includes all functions and actions used by a Workflow Lineage, including any added in the future, without requiring manual updates. Similarly, functions and actions removed from a Workflow Lineage will automatically stop being monitored.

cached_entries.title: Browser locale selection for notification attachments
cached_entries.description: You can now select a browser locale for notification attachments. This allows documents to be rendered according to specific localization settings. The dropdown menu defaults to your current locale on creation and displays the existing value for previously created attachments.

cached_entries.title: Pass properties as inputs to geometry style functions in Ontology Manager
cached_entries.description: Ontology Manager now allows you to pass properties as inputs to object type rendering functions for geometry styles. This capability enables more customized per-object rendering behavior based on specific properties. When you save a function with property inputs, they are saved to the object type's default styles and can be inherited in Gaia.

cached_entries.title: Improved JSON editing in Slate widget Raw tab
cached_entries.description: The **Raw** tab in the Slate widget editor now provides enhanced JSON editing capabilities. The editor displays JSON errors more clearly and only enables the **Update** option when changes have been made and there are no errors. Additionally, interaction properties with excessively long values now include a **Show more** option that allows users to expand and collapse the full content.

cached_entries.title: Faster `if/else` variable transforms
cached_entries.description: Workshop now processes `if-else` variable transforms more efficiently. Instead of waiting for all upstream variables to resolve before proceeding, the runtime evaluates the condition as soon as the necessary inputs are available. This optimization can reduce computation time by up to a second for each `if-else` variable evaluation.

cached_entries.title: Fixed timestamp display in XY charts
cached_entries.description: Workshop now correctly displays timestamps in XY charts by treating them as categorical values rather than continuous numerical values. This ensures timestamps appear properly formatted instead of being rendered as numbers on the Y-axis.

cached_entries.title: Display text inside PDF highlight annotations
cached_entries.description: Workshop's PDF viewer now supports displaying text inside highlight annotations. When configured, the PDF viewer can show text values from a selected property directly within highlighted regions on the document, rather than just showing colored rectangles. This makes annotations more informative by allowing important text to be visible directly on the document.

cached_entries.title: Custom widgets section added to module editor
cached_entries.description: Workshop now includes a dedicated section for custom widgets in the module editor, making it easier to manage and track widgets used in your application. The new section displays the number of custom widgets in your module, provides links to documentation, shows widget titles and usage locations, and indicates when newer versions are available.

cached_entries.title: Iceberg catalog now supports all-or-nothing transactions
cached_entries.description: Foundry's Iceberg catalog now provides all-or-nothing transaction semantics for jobs that perform multiple writes. All table writes are wrapped in a single transaction that either commits fully or is discarded entirely, preventing partial updates from persisting if a job fails mid-run. This matches the existing behaviour of Foundry catalog dataset transactions and ensures that pipelines with multiple outputs remain in a consistent state on failure and retry. Learn more about [Iceberg transactions in Foundry.](/foundry/iceberg/transactions/)

cached_entries.title: GPT-5.4 Mini and GPT-5.4 Nano are now available in AIP
cached_entries.description: GPT-5.4 mini and GPT-5.4 nano are now available directly from OpenAI for non-georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Enforce default preview behavior in Pipeline Builder
cached_entries.description: You can now configure Pipeline Builder to require manual confirmation before running previews, preventing unintended runs and saving compute resources. To enable this setting, navigate to **Control Panel > Pipeline Builder**, and toggle the **Enable preview confirmation by default** option. With this new option, enrollment administrators can choose whether previews run automatically or require manual confirmation for all users in an enrollment. User preference settings in Pipeline Builder remain available, allowing individuals to override the default if they wish.

cached_entries.title: Health checks are now available for virtual and Iceberg tables
cached_entries.description: You can now configure [health checks](/foundry/health-checks/overview/) for [virtual](/foundry/data-integration/virtual-tables/) as well as [managed and virtual Iceberg tables](/foundry/iceberg/managed-virtual/) to enable monitoring and alerting for common issues, such as primary key, schema, and time since last updated checks. Additionally, this functionality extends to virtual tables sourced from [Databricks](/foundry/available-connectors/databricks/#virtual-tables), [Snowflake](/foundry/available-connectors/snowflake/#virtual-tables), and [BigQuery](/foundry/available-connectors/bigquery/#virtual-tables).

cached_entries.title: Improved timestamp handling in Model Studio model inference
cached_entries.description: Model Studio now handles timestamps more consistently during model inference. When your training data has a `Timestamp` column type with daily or multiple-day frequency, the model output will now correctly return a `Timestamp` instead of a `Date`. Additionally, if your input data for inference includes a `Timestamp` with a timezone, the model will now preserve that timezone in its output.

cached_entries.title: Visualize, monitor, and debug your automation workflows with Autopilot
cached_entries.description: As teams chain together automations, logic functions, and actions in Foundry, understanding how those systems behave becomes difficult. [Autopilot](/foundry/autopilot/overview/), now available in beta, provides the visibility needed to visualize how automations connect, trace objects through your workflow, and debug failures in one place. As a beta product, functionality and appearance may change during active development.

cached_entries.title: AIP Analyst: Persist sessions in workshop on unmount
cached_entries.description: AIP Analyst chat sessions in Workshop will now persist when the workshop tab changes or the section containing the chat is collapsed. The PR also provides a reset button for returning to an empty state.

cached_entries.title: Support target branch selection
cached_entries.description: AIP Evals now has branch selection support for the evals run configuration, allowing users to run evaluation suites against specific Foundry branches (not just main). Note that only Logic global branches are currently supported, not functions global branches.

cached_entries.title: AIP Logic now supports target branch selection
cached_entries.description: AIP Logic now has branch selection support for the evals run configuration, allowing users to run evaluation suites against specific Foundry branches (not just main). Note that only Logic global branches are currently supported, not functions global branches.

cached_entries.title: Promote critical object types for improved discoverability
cached_entries.description: Ontology admins can now promote an object type to mark it as a core, critical resource. Promoted object types will be annotated with a purple "verified" checkmark and will appear higher in search results in applications across the platform, including Object Explorer, Gaia, AIP Logic, Slate, and Workshop. Favorited object types will also appear more prominently in search results. See the [status documentation](/foundry/object-link-types/metadata-statuses) for more details.

cached_entries.title: App Pairing now available in Vertex
cached_entries.description: Vertex now supports [App Pairing](/foundry/cross-app-interactivity/app-pairing/), enabling real-time, cross-application workflows between Vertex and other platform applications. Both the standalone Vertex application and the Vertex Workshop widget can now be paired with other supported applications, including AIP Agents and Workshop modules. Paired agents and applications can execute [commands](/foundry/cross-app-interactivity/commands-overview/) directly on Vertex.

cached_entries.title: New color grouping layout in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: You can now organize your nodes by color groupings in Workflow Lineage! This new layout clusters nodes of the same color together, arranging them in a clear, grid-like pattern for easier visualization and analysis. To use the color layout, first select your nodes (use CMD + A to select all). Then, select "Lay out nodes" and choose "Color Layout" (or use the J key shortcut).

cached_entries.title: Carbon "What's New" sidebar will no longer display archived updates
cached_entries.description: The Carbon "What's New" sidebar will no longer display archived updates. Previously, archived updates would appear alongside active ones; now only active (non-archived) updates are shown.

cached_entries.title: Boost RAG accuracy with chunking and embedding in AIP Document Intelligence
cached_entries.description: AIP Document Intelligence now supports chunking and embedding extracted text across all enrollments. Alongside document extraction powered by vision language models and OCR, you can now process documents end-to-end directly within the platform. Chunking is a critical step in document-centric workflows — it determines the granularity of text passed to models in RAG systems, directly impacting retrieval accuracy and downstream generation quality. The new chunking strategy is optimized for Markdown and handles complex structures such as bullet points and tables, improving on the existing raw-text chunking available in Pipeline Builder and AIP Logic. Access this capability through AIP Document Intelligence's text extraction workflow, or deploy it via Python transforms with an option to generate embeddings to support RAG-based workflows.

cached_entries.title: Enforce incremental execution in Pipeline Builder
cached_entries.description: You can now ensure incremental execution in your pipelines with the **Require incremental execution** setting in Pipeline Builder. With this setting enabled, jobs configured to run incrementally will automatically fail if they cannot do so. This helps prevent accidentally snapshotted inputs, forced snapshots from output schema changes, and other unintended snapshot scenarios.

cached_entries.title: Share control of your branches with role-based security
cached_entries.description: Branch creators can now assign users roles to control who can manage and merge their Foundry branches. Previously, only the branch creator could manage and merge a branch. With role-based security, branch creators can delegate ownership to other users and groups, removing bottlenecks while maintaining control over who can access and manage the branch.

cached_entries.title: Maps terminology and labels updated
cached_entries.description: The Maps documentation has been updated to align with the current user interface. References to "Geometries" have been replaced with "Displays" throughout the documentation to match the terminology used in the application. Additionally, the labels section has been updated to reflect recent enhancements to label functionality, and screenshots have been refreshed to show the current user interface.

cached_entries.title: Recently used functions in AIP Assist menu
cached_entries.description: The AIP Assist functions menu in Notepad now displays your three most recently used functions. Choosing a recently used function will run it with the selected content using your saved input format. The output format matches the input format by default but can be changed when inserting the content into Notepad.

cached_entries.title: Recently used functions in Edit with AIP popover
cached_entries.description: You can now quickly access your three most recently used functions in the **Edit with AIP** popover in Notepad. This makes it easier to reuse functions you work with frequently.

cached_entries.title: Ontology SQL card for querying object sets in Quiver
cached_entries.description: Quiver now includes an [Ontology SQL card](/foundry/quiver/card-ontology-sql) that allows you to write SQL queries against object sets in your analysis. Using standard SQL syntax, you can select, filter, join, and aggregate object data to produce tabular results directly in Quiver.

cached_entries.title: Upgraded branch security enabled for all users
cached_entries.description: Security for Foundry Branches is now based on roles, and users and groups can be made owner of a branch, allowing them to administer and merge it. [Learn more about branch security.](/foundry/foundry-branching/branch-security)

cached_entries.title: Interface parameters supported in Automate actions
cached_entries.description: Automate now supports interface parameters in actions. You can pass objects that implement an interface to actions that accept that interface as a parameter type. This includes single interface references, object sets of interfaces, and reference lists of interfaces.

cached_entries.title: Configure default styles for interfaces with geometry properties in Ontology Manager
cached_entries.description: Ontology Manager now allows you to configure default styles for interfaces with geometry properties. This enhancement extends the recent capability to add interfaces to Gaia maps as layers.

cached_entries.title: New checkpoint types for iframe attributes in custom widgets
cached_entries.description: New checkpoint types are now available for iframe attributes in custom widgets. These checkpoints are triggered when an application builder opts-in to additional iframe attributes declared by a custom widget developer including: allow `camera`; allow `microphone`; `allow-downloads`.

cached_entries.title: Enable additional iframe attributes
cached_entries.description: Custom widget developers can now request specific browser features like camera access and popup windows that are restricted by default. The builder in the host application (for example, Workshop) must then manually opt-in to the requested attribute values for them to take effect. Checkpoint types for some attribute values can be defined to capture justification and maintain auditability.

cached_entries.title: The AI forward deployed engineer is now generally available
cached_entries.description: AI FDE, the AI forward deployed engineer, is now generally available for enrollments with AIP enabled. AI FDE allows you to operate Foundry with natural language, using conversations to unlock the power of the Palantir platform. AI FDE makes platform interactions more intuitive and accessible for all users, regardless of technical expertise, while maintaining complete control and visibility into tool use and data access.

cached_entries.title: GPT-5.4 is now available in AIP
cached_entries.description: GPT-5.4 is now available directly from OpenAI and Azure for non-georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now available in AIP
cached_entries.description: Gemini 3.1 Flash-Lite is now available directly from Google VertexAI for non-georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Output files and file sets in Pipeline Builder
cached_entries.description: Pipeline Builder now supports outputting files and file sets. After transforming your file input, select the transform you want to output, then choose **Add output** and **Output file**. Security markings can also be configured on file outputs.

cached_entries.title: Python transforms preview in Visual Studio Code now support media sets
cached_entries.description: You can now preview media sets in Visual Studio Code workspaces. All media set inputs are supported in preview mode, while for outputs, only the `put_media_item` operation is currently supported. Note that incremental preview is not supported for media sets.

cached_entries.title: Monitoring status color mode in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now includes the **Monitoring status** color mode, which visualizes the status of monitors for resources in your graph. When enabled, nodes are colored based on the highest severity alert from monitors you have access to, showing whether resources are healthy or have high, medium, or low severity warnings. Note that resources may have additional alerts that are not visible to you, depending on your permissions. This color mode also highlights nodes that are eligible for monitoring but do not have monitoring configured, making it easier to identify gaps in your monitoring coverage.

cached_entries.title: New shortcuts from Gaia maps to Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: You can now add Gaia map resources as nodes in Workflow Lineage by using the shortcut `Cmd + I` (macOS) or `Ctrl + I` (Windows). Alternatively, you can also add Gaia maps as nodes by selecting **Add > Add to Workflow Lineage** in the **File** menu when viewing a Gaia map.

cached_entries.title: Last reindexed color mode in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now includes a color mode that visualizes when objects were last reindexed in Object Storage v2. This visualization helps you identify objects that were reindexed within the past day, week, month, or year, as well as objects that failed reindexing or have never been indexed. The exact reindex date is displayed beneath each node on the graph.

cached_entries.title: Enter a custom prompt when editing a Notepad document with AIP
cached_entries.description: You can now enter a custom prompt when editing a [Notepad document](/foundry/notepad/overview/) with AIP.

cached_entries.title: Improved Properties tab availability in Quiver time series plots
cached_entries.description: The **Properties** tab in Quiver time series plot editors has been improved to appear consistently across more plot types, show properties of all objects backing the series, and provide a link to open the object view in a new tab.

cached_entries.title: Access replica history in Compute Modules
cached_entries.description: You can now access replica history for your compute modules to debug the status of all replicas from any run over the last 7 days. View metrics, logs, replica lifespans, and termination reasons to gain insights and troubleshoot recent runs.

cached_entries.title: Improved visibility of newly created branches in branch selectors
cached_entries.description: Newly created Foundry Branches now appear immediately in branch selectors. Previously, branches could take up to five minutes to appear after creation.

cached_entries.title: Debug page for fired monitor alerts in Troubleshoot Alerts tab
cached_entries.description: The **Troubleshoot alerts** tab now includes a dedicated debug page for fired monitor alerts. This page displays the monitor's alert history over the past 30 days, along with the firing value, the threshold value, and a full breakdown of all severity conditions. The alert debug page is currently available for function and action types, with additional sections and support for more resource types planned in future updates.

cached_entries.title: Updated Object Explorer landing page for ontology discoverability
cached_entries.description: The Object Explorer landing page has been redesigned to improve discoverability, with clearer organization of object types and more prominent display of promoted and favorited items.

cached_entries.title: New Object Explorer shortcut
cached_entries.description: A new **Ontology** shortcut is now pinned to the top of the Foundry navigation bar, providing direct access to Object Explorer as the primary tool for ontology search and analysis.

cached_entries.title: Object storage indexing status visualization in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now includes a color mode that visualizes Object Storage v2 indexing status for object type nodes. When enabled, nodes are colored based on whether their last indexing job was successful or failed, with the color intensity indicating how recently the event occurred. Each object type node displays a timestamp showing exactly when the most recent indexing event took place, making it easier to monitor and troubleshoot your object storage indexing status at a glance.

cached_entries.title: Model lifecycle stages visible in Workflow Lineage search
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now displays lifecycle stage information for models in search results. You can quickly identify which models are experimental, deprecated, or sunset directly in the search interface, making it easier to choose the appropriate models for your workflows.

cached_entries.title: Improved media item picker in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Workshop now offers an improved picker for media items, making it easier to select and work with media content. Media items are also now set as the default option when configuring media previews.

cached_entries.title: Improved filter options for string properties in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Workshop now displays filter component menus for all string properties, including those that do not support exact matching. Previously, these menus were hidden entirely. For properties without exact match support, the menu now shows the **Keyword** search option while other options appear disabled with a helpful tooltip. Additionally, derived properties now support keyword search, and the filter interface indicates whether a property is indexed for regex when the **Keyword** option is selected.

cached_entries.title: Object type cards display variable reference counts
cached_entries.description: Workshop now displays the number of variables that reference each object type directly on the object type cards in the overview sidebar. This makes it easier for module builders to understand how object types are being used throughout their application without having to search for references manually.

cached_entries.title: Update Workshop's documentation to cover new features and ensure style consistency
cached_entries.description:
Documentation for Workshop has been updated with new content covering features such as: 

- [Triggering actions from the Media Uploader widget](/foundry/workshop/widgets-media-uploader/)
- [Controlling auto-refresh updates with events](/foundry/workshop/auto-refresh/#control-auto-refresh-updates-with-events)
- [Using the **Changelog** panel during rebasing](/foundry/workshop/changelog/#using-the-changelog-panel-during-rebasing)
- [Chaining an event with an action](/foundry/workshop/widgets-button-group/#chaining-an-event-with-an-action)

Additionally, the updates include improved guidance in [Workshop's introductory tutorial](/foundry/workshop/getting-started/) as well as terminology and style consistency improvements throughout the application's documentation.

cached_entries.title: Inline image attachments now supported in Automate
cached_entries.description: You can now choose how attachments appear in emails sent from automations. When adding attachments to emails in Automate, a new toggle now allows attachments to be rendered as inline images instead of PDFs. This provides more control over email presentation.

cached_entries.title: Project selection available during installation configuration
cached_entries.description: Users can now select projects during the Marketplace installation configuration phase rather than during the installation job. This provides the flexibility to choose an existing project or create a new one using the standardized project creation flow. Additionally, users can lock projects during installation configuration to map project roles to Marketplace roles, and unlock projects from either the Compass access side panel or installation settings.

cached_entries.title: Improved code editor theming and accessibility
cached_entries.description: Code editors throughout Slate now use a dedicated light theme with improved color contrast and broader syntax coverage. The affected editors include JavaScript (Functions, Libraries, Events/Actions), CSS/LESS (Styles), SQL (SQL/Foundry Datasource queries), and JSON (HTTPJSON queries, Function inputs). The improvements include better readability with consistent syntax coloring, colors that meet WCAG 2.1 AA contrast requirements, expanded language support with more token types, and refreshed Handlebars template syntax using a purple-toned palette.

cached_entries.title: Customizable security display option for property lists
cached_entries.description: Workshop now offers customizable display options for security information in property lists. You can toggle a setting in the widget panel to show a compact or responsive icon format, saving space in your application's interface while still providing property-specific security context.

cached_entries.title: Fixed conditional coloring in metric cards using different object properties
cached_entries.description: Workshop metric cards now correctly apply dynamic conditional coloring rules when the color is based on a different property than the one being displayed. Previously, coloring rules that referenced a property other than the displayed value (such as coloring by `name` while displaying `age`) would fail to apply.

cached_entries.title: Introducing Pilot, Foundry's AI-powered tool for building React OSDK applications
cached_entries.description: Pilot is an AI-powered application builder that lets you create full-stack applications on top of your ontology using natural language prompts. Pilot will be available in beta starting the week of March 9. To use Pilot, describe the application you want to build, and Pilot will generate the ontology, design, and front-end code in an isolated workspace with no manual data wiring or UI coding required.

cached_entries.title: Generate notional data with LLMs in Pipeline Builder
cached_entries.description: You can now use LLMs to generate richer, more flexible datasets in manually entered tables in Pipeline Builder. Describe the data you want, reference other columns in your prompt for dynamic generation, and preview up to 10 rows of LLM-generated data before applying changes to your full table. You can also lock and unlock columns to control which data gets regenerated and which stays the same. These two new features are now available on all enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Track your Workshop applications with usage metrics
cached_entries.description: Workshop now includes a built-in Metrics tab in the editor sidebar, giving module builders direct visibility into how their applications are being used. Usage metrics track two categories of data—action submissions and layout views—so builders can understand which parts of their module are most active and identify trends over time. All metrics are aggregate counts and are not attributable to any specific user.

cached_entries.title: AI FDE new change mode and request clarification skills
cached_entries.description: AI FDE now includes two new skills: Change mode and Request clarification. AI FDE can switch to a different mode mid-task when the work requires it, without needing you to switch manually. It can also ask follow-up questions—using either multiple-choice or free-text prompts—when it needs more information before proceeding.

cached_entries.title: New "manage skills" skill
cached_entries.description: AI FDE now has a skill called **Manage skills** that allows it to tidy its working memory and turn skills on or off as needed, enabling it to better adapt to the workflow at hand.

cached_entries.title: Notepad integration with AI FDE
cached_entries.description: You can now read and write Notepad documents directly from AI FDE. This integration enables you to work with Notepad documents as part of your AI FDE workflows.

cached_entries.title: Improved object type differentiation in Data Lineage resource selector
cached_entries.description: Data Lineage now displays ontology names in the subtitle when multiple object types share the same display name across different ontologies. For example, if you have two object types both named "Customer" from different ontologies, you'll see the ontology name in the subtitle to help differentiate them. If an object type name is unique or duplicates are from the same ontology, only the object type ID will be shown as before.

cached_entries.title: Improved export operations in Peer Manager
cached_entries.description:
When downloading a peering job's update payload as an XML file, a `PEER_MANAGER_CDS_PAYLOAD_EXPORT` checkpoint type will now be triggered instead of the generic `FRONTEND_EXPORT` checkpoint type. When downloading object type schemas as XSD files for cross-domain connections, a `PEER_MANAGER_OBJECT_TYPE_SCHEMAS_EXPORT` checkpoint type will now be triggered instead of the `FRONTEND_EXPORT` checkpoint type.

For more details on these checkpoint types, see Peer Manager checkpoint types documentation in [Checkpoint types](https://www.palantir.com/docs/foundry/checkpoints/checkpoint-types).

cached_entries.title: Configure visibility and text of action toast messages in Ontology Manager
cached_entries.description: Ontology Manager now allows you to customize the visibility and text of toast messages that appear when actions are executed. You can configure actions to hide the success message toast, customize the in-progress message (default is "Applying edits..."), or completely hide the in-progress toast. This enhancement is particularly useful for Workshop applications where the default toast messages might not align with the user's conceptual understanding of the action being performed.

cached_entries.title: New peering checkpoint types
cached_entries.description: New checkpoint types `PEER_MANAGER_CDS_PAYLOAD_EXPORT` and `PEER_MANAGER_OBJECT_TYPE_SCHEMAS_EXPORT` for exports initiated by users in Peer Manager are now available for use. See [checkpoint types documentation](/foundry/checkpoints/checkpoint-types/).

cached_entries.title: Fixed role grant display when hovering in project permissions
cached_entries.description: Role grant changes in the project permissions panel now remain visible when hovering over roles. Previously, proposed role changes would disappear when hovering over them before saving, making it difficult to review permission changes.

cached_entries.title: Carbon workspaces now moved to trash instead of permanently deleted
cached_entries.description: Workspaces in Carbon are now moved to the trash instead of being permanently deleted when using the workspace deletion option. This change allows you to recover workspaces from the trash in Compass if they were deleted accidentally.

cached_entries.title: Customizable action notification messages
cached_entries.description: Workshop now provides more control over the notification messages that appear when actions are executed. You can now hide or customize both the in-progress message ("Applying edits...") and the success message that appears after an action completes. This is particularly useful for creating seamless user experiences in applications where the default messaging does not match the conceptual model of what the action is doing.

cached_entries.title: GPT-5.3 Codex now available in AIP
cached_entries.description: GPT-5.3 Codex is now available directly from OpenAI for non-georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Perform time series analysis with a dedicated workspace in Quiver
cached_entries.description: Quiver now includes a redesigned analysis creation experience, making it easier to choose the right workspace for your task. When creating a new analysis, you can now select from three analysis types: Quiver analysis, time series analysis, and object set path analysis. As part of this update, we are introducing the [**time series analysis workspace**](/foundry/quiver/analysis-types/#time-series-analysis/), a dedicated interface purpose-built for ad-hoc time series analysis. It provides a streamlined environment for visualizing and comparing time series data without the full complexity of a Quiver analysis, making it accessible to a wider range of users. When a more advanced workspace is needed, a time series analysis can be opened directly in Quiver. Additionally, the [**Time Series Analysis widget**]( /docs/foundry/workshop/widget-time-series-analysis/) brings the same interface and tooling to Workshop, allowing application builders to embed time series analysis directly in their applications. The widget includes fine-grained configuration options to tailor the experience for operational users.

cached_entries.title: New Observability documentation category added
cached_entries.description: A new top-level Observability category has been added to the documentation sidebar, consolidating content that was previously split across Data Integration and Workflow Lineage sections. This category includes health checks, monitoring views, and Workflow Lineage observability content. The reorganization improves navigation while maintaining all existing URLs through permanent redirects.

cached_entries.title: Expanded Workflow Lineage access across the platform
cached_entries.description: You can now open Workflow Lineage graphs from more locations across the platform using the `Cmd+i` (macOS) or `Ctrl+i` (Windows) shortcut, as well as a dedicated navigation option available on various resource types. Leverage this feature to better explore and understand your workflows from different applications across the Palantir ecosystem.

cached_entries.title: Set maximum duration for streaming pipelines in Pipeline Builder
cached_entries.description: Pipeline Builder now allows you to set a maximum duration for streaming pipeline jobs. When deploying a streaming pipeline, you can specify a maximum runtime between one minute and seven days using the new Max Duration setting in the deployment menu. For non-main branches, this setting is enabled by default with a one day duration limit.

cached_entries.title: Function and action monitors now support Workshop and third-party application scopes
cached_entries.description: Function and action monitors now support two new dynamic scopes; Workshop and third-party applications. You can set up a monitor that automatically includes all functions and actions used by an application, including any added in the future, without requiring manual updates. Similarly, functions and actions removed from an application will automatically stop being monitored.

cached_entries.title: Monitor status visualization in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now includes a color mode that visualizes monitoring status for resources in your graph. When enabled, nodes are colored based on the maximum severity of their firing monitors or marked as healthy if all monitors are passing. The visualization also highlights which nodes are eligible for monitoring but do not have monitoring configured.

cached_entries.title: Improved variable search behavior in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Workshop now automatically scrolls to the top of the variable list when you modify a search query. This ensures that all matching variables are visible, preventing results from being hidden when the list was previously scrolled down.

cached_entries.title: Pivot transform table in Quiver
cached_entries.description: The [pivot transform table](/foundry/quiver/card-pivot-transform-table/) card is now available in Quiver, allowing you to group and aggregate data from a transform table using customizable row and column properties with flexible aggregation options (such as totals, counts, and unique values).

cached_entries.title: Models shipped through Marketplace now support `use_sidecar=True` Transforms annotation
cached_entries.description: Model assets can now be packaged in Marketplace products when they are used as a sidecar `ModelInput` in transforms. This enables sharing models as part of Marketplace products when referenced in transforms.

cached_entries.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 now available for UK enrollments
cached_entries.description: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now available from AWS Bedrock on UK georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Compass-managed permissions for Developer Console applications
cached_entries.description: Developer Console applications can now inherit permissions from their parent project or folder using Compass-managed user permissions. Learn more in the [Developer Console documentation](/foundry/developer-console/permissions). Support for CBAC enrollments is in active development and expected to roll out at the end of March. Prior to this change, Developer Console users were automatically set as the `Owner` of the applications they created and had to manually share each application with other users and groups from the **Sharing & tokens** section of their application. This legacy model required per-application permission management.

cached_entries.title: New files landing page and the ability to promote files
cached_entries.description: Starting the week of February 23, a redesigned Files landing page, along with the introduction of a Promoted status, will be available across all enrollments. These changes are designed to help users find relevant content as the volume of resources on the platform increases.

cached_entries.title: Custom background colors for Workshop sections and pages
cached_entries.description: Workshop sections and pages now support custom background colors, giving application builders better control over the visual design of their modules. In addition to the existing preset shades, you can now apply any hex color or Blueprint color to section and page backgrounds. Saved colors defined at the module level are also available as section and page background options, making it straightforward to maintain a consistent color palette across an application.

cached_entries.title: North-of-ontology log search available in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now allows you to search across all north-of-ontology service logs produced by a resource over the past seven days. Log search aggregates logs from every execution, making it easy to investigate recurring errors or track down intermittent issues.

cached_entries.title: Microsoft Word export support in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Workshop now supports exporting Microsoft Word (DOCX) files via function-backed exports, allowing you to download documents in a widely compatible format for offline use, review, or distribution.

cached_entries.title: Gemini 3.1 Pro now available via VertexAI
cached_entries.description: Gemini 3.1 Pro model is now available from VertexAI on commercial, non-georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Claude Sonnet 4.6 now available from Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, and Google Vertex
cached_entries.description: Claude Sonnet 4.6 is now available from Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, and Google Vertex on non-georestricted enrollments. For US and EU georestricted enrollments, this model is available from AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex.

cached_entries.title: Open in Workflow Lineage option for Notepad documents
cached_entries.description: You can now open a Notepad document in Workflow Lineage directly from Notepad. This feature is available through the **Open in Workflow Lineage** option in the **File** menu dropdown or by using the keyboard shortcut `Cmd+i` (Mac) or `Ctrl+i` (Windows) .

cached_entries.title: Allow searching for markings by marking ID
cached_entries.description: Pipeline Builder now allows you to search for an output's markings by the marking's ID in addition to its name. To search for a marking, choose **Configure markings** in **Pipeline outputs** to render the **Security markings** modal, where you can select the configuration icon and choose **Search by ID** before entering the marking's ID in the **Search all markings IDs** search bar.

cached_entries.title: Function effects supported in Automate
cached_entries.description: You can now run functions when an [automation](/foundry/automate/overview/) triggers or recovers, enabling you to automatically analyze data, transform inputs, and return completed results. [Learn more about function effects in Automate](/foundry/automate/effect-function).

cached_entries.title: Filter trashed resources from Workflow Lineage graphs
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now includes a trash icon in its toolbar that enables you to remove trashed resources from your graph and exclude them from node connections. This allows you to focus on active resources and simplifies navigation, especially when working with complex workflows containing many nodes. You can add the trashed resources back to your graph by selecting the trash icon again.

cached_entries.title: Automatically obfuscate sensitive data in Sensitive Data Scanner using Cipher
cached_entries.description: Sensitive Data Scanner now supports a new Match Action type, [**Obfuscate Data**](/foundry/sensitive-data-scanner/create-match-actions/create-an-obfuscate-data-match-action/), allowing you to automatically encrypt or hash matched sensitive data using [Cipher](/foundry/cipher/overview/). This addition joins the existing Match Action types, **Apply Markings** and **Create Issues**, giving data governance users more direct control over how sensitive data is handled when a match is detected.

cached_entries.title: Custom widgets available in Workshop mobile
cached_entries.description: Custom widgets can now be used in Workshop mobile applications. Users can access and interact with custom widgets when using Workshop on mobile devices.

cached_entries.title: Custom widgets available in Workshop mobile
cached_entries.description: Custom widgets can now be used in Workshop mobile applications. Developers should ensure their widgets are compatible with mobile layouts for optimal user experience. Future updates will improve search and discovery features to allow filtering for mobile-compatible custom widgets.

cached_entries.title: Media references supported in function-backed columns
cached_entries.description: Function-backed columns in Workshop now properly display media references as image previews instead of showing "unsupported type." This applies to both single media references and arrays of media references, matching the behavior of standard media reference columns.

cached_entries.title: Improved accessibility for scrollable lists
cached_entries.description: Workshop now provides better screen reader support for scrollable lists of items. This accessibility improvement ensures more accurate semantic structure and clearer announcements when navigating through lists with assistive technologies.

cached_entries.title: Improved pivot table width behavior
cached_entries.description: Pivot tables in Workshop now properly expand to fill available space when the parent container width changes while the **Fill Width** option is enabled. Previously, pivot tables would only adjust their width when interacted with directly, leaving empty space when container dimensions changed.

cached_entries.title: Open in Workflow Lineage option added to Quiver
cached_entries.description: You can now open your Quiver dashboard in Workflow Lineage directly from the **File** menu. This new option displays the Quiver dashboard node and its directly related nodes in a Workflow Lineage graph. You can also use the keyboard shortcut `Cmd+i` (Mac) or `Ctrl+i` (Windows)  to quickly access this functionality.

cached_entries.title: Add support for secret rotation checkpoint
cached_entries.description: The **Rotate OAuth client secret** checkpoint type is now available. Users can now configure checkpoints to appear when rotating the secret of an OAuth client in [Developer Console](/foundry/developer-console/overview/).

cached_entries.title: Open Machinery processes in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: You can now open Machinery processes in Workflow Lineage directly from the Machinery application. Access this functionality through the new **Open in Workflow Lineage** option in the **File** menu dropdown, or by using the keyboard shortcut `ctrl + i` (Windows) or `cmd + i` (macOS).

cached_entries.title: Create materializations for object types in Ontology Manager
cached_entries.description: Ontology Manager now supports creating materializations for object types. This feature allows you to define how object data should be materialized. Currently, this functionality is limited to materializations on the main branch only.

cached_entries.title: View inherited link type constraints for interfaces in Ontology Manager
cached_entries.description: Ontology Manager now displays which link type constraints are inherited when working with interfaces that extend other interfaces. This information is visible both on the **Extensions** tab and when previewing a new extension, making it easier to understand inheritance relationships between interfaces.

cached_entries.title: Search for Gaia maps in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description:
You can now search for Gaia maps directly in the left search panel of Workflow Lineage. Previously, Gaia map nodes could only be accessed by expanding from existing nodes on the graph. This enhancement makes it easier to find and incorporate Gaia maps into your workflow visualizations. 

**Note:** To search for Gaia maps in Workflow Lineage, your enrollment must use both Foundry and Gotham.

cached_entries.title: Enhanced interface details panel in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now provides more comprehensive information in the interface details panel. When viewing an interface, you can now see its properties, the object types that implement it, and the interfaces it extends from. This additional context makes it easier to understand interface relationships and structure within your ontology.

cached_entries.title: Model selection now available during Marketplace installation
cached_entries.description: You can now select models during product installation in Marketplace rather than packaging them with your pipeline. This new model selector in the installation app allows you to choose the appropriate model version at installation time, reducing the size of packaged products while ensuring that dependent blocks receive the exact model version they require.

cached_entries.title: Model selection available during Marketplace product installation
cached_entries.description: Users can now select models during product installation in Marketplace. This eliminates the need to package models when shipping pipelines, providing more flexibility and potentially reducing package size. The new model selector allows users to resolve specific model versions required by dependent components.

cached_entries.title: Open in Data Lineage option in File menu
cached_entries.description: Slate now includes an "Open in Data Lineage" option in the File dropdown menu. This option opens a new Data Lineage graph displaying the Slate node and its directly related nodes. Application builders can also use the keyboard shortcut `Ctrl+i` (Windows) or `Cmd+i` (Mac) to access this functionality.

cached_entries.title: Variable list in Workshop now shows variables used in active page
cached_entries.description: Workshop's variable list now includes a partition for variables used in the active page, in addition to the existing partition for variables used in the selected widget. Only one partition is shown at a time, with the widget partition taking precedence when a widget is selected.

cached_entries.title: Consistent simplified user display across Workshop widgets
cached_entries.description: Workshop now displays user information consistently across all widgets. Object Dropdown, Object List, and Property List widgets now use the same simplified user display as Object Tables, showing only essential user information in popovers instead of the full user profile with additional details.

cached_entries.title: Core Object Views are now available
cached_entries.description: Foundry now automatically creates a core [Object View](/foundry/object-views/overview/) for a new object type to provide a standardized representation of all its objects, ensuring other users have a holistic understanding of its schema and links without requiring additional configuration. Core Object Views provide a consistent, complete display of object data after object type creation, and they are now generally available across Foundry enrollments. [Learn more about core Object Views](/foundry/object-views/core-object-views/).

cached_entries.title: You can now view performance metrics for your Logic functions in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: You can now view execution and p95 metrics for your Logic functions directly in Workflow Lineage in the bottom **Function metrics** panel.

cached_entries.title: Markdown editing mode now available in the text input widget
cached_entries.description: Workshop text input widgets now support Markdown editing mode, providing a rich text formatting toolbar and the ability to toggle between rich text and raw Markdown views. Enable this feature by selecting the Markdown format option in the widget's configuration panel.

cached_entries.title: You can now view performance metrics of your Logic in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: You can now view execution and p95 metrics for your Logic functions directly in Workflow Lineage in the bottom **Function metrics** panel.

cached_entries.title: Image resizing in Flow Capture
cached_entries.description: Flow Capture now allows you to resize images and have those changes persist in documentation. Resized images also carry over to Notepad and PDF exports.

cached_entries.title: Flow Capture prevents accidental recording interruptions when opening links
cached_entries.description: Flow Capture now intercepts links that would open in new tabs during recording sessions. When clicking a link that would normally open in a new tab (via target="_blank" or cmd/ctrl+click), users are presented with a dialog offering two options: open in a new tab (exiting the recording context) or open in the current tab (continuing the recording). This prevents accidental loss of recording context when navigating between pages.

cached_entries.title: Open Pipeline Builder objects in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: You can now open Pipeline Builder object types directly in Workflow Lineage from within Pipeline Builder. This integration allows for seamless navigation between your pipeline definitions and their visualization in the Workflow Lineage graph.

cached_entries.title: Historical resource queue utilization view in Resource Management
cached_entries.description: Resource Management now provides visibility into historical resource queue utilization. You can view the usage patterns of vCPUs or vGPUs for any queue over the past 7 days, allowing you to monitor utilization trends and identify usage spikes. This historical view helps determine if your queues are appropriately sized for your workloads and assists with troubleshooting resource-related issues.

cached_entries.title: Model functions now supported in TypeScript v2 and Python functions
cached_entries.description: To use model functions in TypeScript v2 and Python repositories, your model functions must be **tied to an ontology**. Starting February 2026, all new model functions will automatically be ontology-bound. Existing space-bound model functions can be migrated from the model user interface. See [Functions on models](/foundry/functions/functions-on-models) for code examples and detailed usage and migration instructions.

cached_entries.title: Function ontology binding displayed in Workflow Lineage sidebar
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now displays ontology binding information in the details sidebar when you select a function. This makes it easier to identify which ontology a function is associated with, providing better context when working with functions across multiple ontologies.

cached_entries.title: Strict mode added to folder tracking in DevOps
cached_entries.description: DevOps now offers a strict mode option for folder tracking. When enabled, strict mode performs additional validations to ensure that only resources from within the designated source folder can be added to your product, preventing accidental inclusion of external resources. You can enable this option in the folder tracking settings of your DevOps product.

cached_entries.title: Keyboard shortcut added to open Workflow Lineage from DevOps Resources Overview
cached_entries.description: You can now use the keyboard shortcut `Cmd+I` (Mac) or `Ctrl+I` (Windows) in the DevOps Resources overview page to quickly open the selected resource in Workflow Lineage. This shortcut provides faster access to workflow visualization and management directly from your DevOps drafts.

cached_entries.title: Keyboard shortcut for Workflow Lineage added to Resources overview
cached_entries.description: Users can now open Workflow Lineage directly from the Resources overview page using the keyboard shortcut `Cmd+i` (Mac) or `Ctrl+i` (Windows).

cached_entries.title: Strict mode added to folder tracking in DevOps
cached_entries.description: DevOps now supports strict mode for folder tracking, which performs additional validations to ensure no resources from outside the source folder can be added to your product. Users can enable strict folder tracking in the folder tracking configuration section when creating a new folder tracked product or editing an existing folder tracked product.

cached_entries.title: Cross-store linked products now available in DevOps
cached_entries.description: DevOps now supports linking stores together, enabling linked products to span across multiple stores. To configure linked stores, navigate to the store settings page and add the store in the **Linked stores** section.

cached_entries.title: Search Workshop variables by ID
cached_entries.description: Workshop now allows you to search for variables by their unique IDs in addition to their display names. Enter a variable ID (found in the JSON configuration of widgets) in the search field to quickly locate specific variables in your modules.

cached_entries.title: Hover-activated add buttons in Workshop layouts
cached_entries.description: Workshop now displays **Add widget** and **Set layout** options only when you hover over sections in your module layouts in edit mode. This better reflects the look of your module when it is published and streamlines the module building process.

cached_entries.title: Monitoring views now support project scopes for ontology resources
cached_entries.description: Monitoring views now support **project scopes for ontology resources**, allowing you to dynamically monitor ontology resources across one or multiple projects. Supported resource types are: object types, many-to-many links, actions, and functions. Any new resources created in or added to the project will now be automatically monitored.

cached_entries.title: Furnace is now the default SQL warehousing engine
cached_entries.description: [Furnace](/foundry/sql-warehousing/furnace) is now rolled out as the default [SQL warehousing](/foundry/sql-warehousing/overview/) engine, replacing the [legacy Spark and Direct Read](/foundry/analytics-connectivity/architecture#execution-engines) engines. Furnace provides a unified SQL experience with intelligent backing engine selection, automatically choosing between Spark and Trino based on workload and performance characteristics, and leverages the [transforms compute rate type](/foundry/sql-warehousing/compute-usage/) under the hood. The new architecture decouples query parsing from execution, enabling consistent behavior across in-platform queries, ODBC/JDBC connections, and Arrow Flight SQL integrations. You can access Furnace SQL from [SQL Console](/foundry/sql-warehousing/sql-console/). This transition is automatic and should not require changes to existing workflows. Reference the [SQL dialect documentation](/foundry/sql-warehousing/sql-dialect/) for syntax details.

cached_entries.title: Unsaved changes confirmation in Flow Capture
cached_entries.description: Flow Capture now displays a confirmation dialog when you attempt to navigate away from the edit page with unsaved changes. This prevents accidental loss of work by giving you the option to stay on the page and save your changes.

cached_entries.title: Data Connection exports are now supported in Marketplace
cached_entries.description: Data Connection exports can now be packaged alongside syncs in Marketplace. In order to add an export to your marketplace product, use the **Add output** option, which will prompt you to select a Data Connection source. The dialog will walk you through and allow you to include syncs as well as exports in your Marketplace product.

cached_entries.title: Improved filter list widget chart behavior
cached_entries.description: Filter list widget charts now maintain their size during loading states, reducing layout shifts. The active filter range remains visible even when no data matches the filter criteria. Dark mode support has also been enhanced with improved contrast.

cached_entries.title: Claude Opus 4.6 available from Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, and Google Vertex
cached_entries.description: Claude Opus 4.6 is now available from Anthropic, AWS Bedrock, and Google Vertex on non-georestricted enrollments. For US and EU non-georestricted enrollments, the model is available from AWS Bedrock and Google Vertex.

cached_entries.title: Model Studio, our no-code model training tool, is now generally available
cached_entries.description: Model Studio, a new workspace that allows users to train and deploy machine learning models, will be generally available the week of February 9. Model Studio transforms the complex task of building production-grade models into a streamlined no-code process that makes advanced machine learning more accessible. Whether you are a data scientist looking to accelerate your workflow, or a business user eager to unlock insights from your data, Model Studio provides essential tools and a user-friendly interface that simplifies the journey from data to model. [Learn more about Model Studio](/foundry/model-studio/overview/).

cached_entries.title: Dataset materializations now supported for object types with row-level policies
cached_entries.description: Dataset materializations are now supported for object types with row-level policies on organizations. This applies for restricted views and object security policies that reference a mandatory control property with a set of allowed organizations.

cached_entries.title: Access Workflow Lineage in Automate with keyboard shortcut and action button
cached_entries.description: You can now open Workflow Lineage from an automation through an action button on the overview page or by using the `cmd+i` (macOS) or `ctrl+i` (Windows) keyboard shortcut.

cached_entries.title: Geospatial map nodes in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now supports geospatial map nodes in workflow visualizations. You can access these nodes through new connection buttons from other nodes, such as object types. This allows you to incorporate geospatial data into your ontology visualizations and better understand relationships between geospatial resources and other ontological elements.

cached_entries.title: Expanded node visualization when opening items in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now automatically displays upstream and downstream nodes when opening a single item. Previously, when opening an Object Type, Action Type, Function, or Logic Function from Ontology Manager, only the selected node would appear in the graph. With this improvement, related nodes are immediately visible, providing better context and reducing the need for manual exploration.

cached_entries.title: Bulk move resources in Data Lineage
cached_entries.description: Data Lineage now supports bulk selection and movement of resources. You can select multiple resources within the same project and move them to a different folder or project in a single operation. When moving resources, the system performs the same permission checks and provides the same warnings as when moving resources in Projects.

cached_entries.title: Project-based coloring for migrated objects in Data Lineage
cached_entries.description: Data Lineage now displays folder, project, and portfolio color modes for object types and action types that have been migrated to projects. This enhancement provides consistent visual representation of project-based permissions across your data lineage graphs.

cached_entries.title: Create unscoped Developer Console applications
cached_entries.description: Developers can now create unscoped Developer Console applications and update the scope of existing applications at any time. Learn more in the [Developer Console documentation](/foundry/developer-console/application-scopes).

cached_entries.title: Customizable node width in Workflow Lineage graphs
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now allows you to adjust node widths for each workflow graph individually. This enhancement makes it easier to read and work with nodes that have longer names by providing better visualization of their complete labels.

cached_entries.title: Presentation mode in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Presentation mode is now available in Workflow Lineage for all enrollments. Use the presentation mode for Workflow Lineage to create and organize visual frames of your workflow graph, making it easier to present your work. To get started, select the project screen icon in the bottom of the graph.

cached_entries.title: Visual indicators for conditionally visible sections in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Workshop now displays icons and tooltips in the layout panel to indicate which sections have conditional visibility enabled. This makes it easier to identify and manage conditionally visible sections, even when they are currently hidden.

cached_entries.title: Review uploaded videos in Flow Capture
cached_entries.description: Flow Capture now supports a complete review phase for uploaded videos. After uploading a prerecorded demo, you can review the extracted screenshots in a thumbnail grid, adjust timestamps, delete unwanted images, and confirm your selections before finalizing the documentation. This completes the video upload workflow, enabling you to use prerecorded demos as a starting point for documentation.

cached_entries.title: Create streaming time series conditions in Automate
cached_entries.description: Users can now evaluate time series conditions at low latency using streaming data.  Review the [streaming alerting](/foundry/time-series/alerting-overview#streaming-alerting) documentation for more information.

cached_entries.title: Function upgrade panels visible without node selection in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now displays function upgrade panels in the bottom bar even when no nodes are selected in your graph. Previously, you needed to select nodes to see bottom panels. With this improvement, function upgrade, model usage and submission criteria upgrade panels  appear automatically, making it easier to identify and apply available upgrades across your workflow.

cached_entries.title: Workshop displays deprecated object types in Module Overview panel
cached_entries.description: Workshop now shows when an object type is marked as deprecated in Ontology Manager directly in the module overview panel, making it easier to identify deprecated ontology entities in your modules.

cached_entries.title: Conditional visibility for metric cards
cached_entries.description: Workshop now supports conditional visibility for metric cards, allowing you to show or hide these components based on variable values or other conditions. This functionality works similarly to conditional visibility for other components like buttons.

cached_entries.title: Execute SQL Query API is now generally available
cached_entries.description: The Execute SQL Query API is now generally available. This API enables programmatic execution of SQL queries against tabular data in Foundry, with support for referencing datasets and tables by path or RID. Learn more about executing SQL queries in the [API documentation](/foundry/api/v2/sql-queries-v2-resources/sql-queries/execute-sql-query).

cached_entries.title: Claude Sonnet 4.5, Claude Haiku 4.5 now available in Japan region
cached_entries.description: Claude Sonnet 4.5 and Claude Haiku 4.5 models are now available from AWS Bedrock on Japan georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Deploy containers with compute modules
cached_entries.description: [Compute modules](/foundry/compute-modules/overview/) are now generally available in Foundry. With compute modules, you can run containers that scale dynamically based on load, bringing your existing code, in any language, into Foundry without rewriting it.

cached_entries.title: The public documentation home page has been redesigned
cached_entries.description: The documentation home page has been redesigned to better integrate content for different audiences. Toggles are now available in the top right of the explore and reference documentation sections, allowing users to choose the content that is most relevant to them and surface content according to capabilities or products.

cached_entries.title: Edit test case parameters directly from table headers
cached_entries.description: AIP Evals now allows you to edit test case parameters directly from table column headers. You can select any column header to modify parameter names, types, and optionality settings inline. A new add column button in the header row makes it easy to add new parameters, and validation errors now appear directly on individual column headers for more precise feedback. The existing schema editor remains available through a minimal icon button when you need to perform more complex operations like column reordering.

cached_entries.title: Edit test case parameters inline in AIP Logic
cached_entries.description: You can now edit test case parameters directly from column headers in AIP Logic. Select any column header to modify parameter names, types, and optionality without opening the full schema editor. A new '+' icon in the header row allows you to quickly add new parameters, and validation errors now appear on individual column headers for easier troubleshooting.

cached_entries.title: Improved link type display when editing permissions in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now distinguishes between link types that will be affected by permission changes and those that will not when using the permissions editing dialog. When editing object type permissions, the dialog separates link types into those that will have their permissions updated (based on your edit permissions) and those that will remain unaffected. This makes it clearer which ontological resources will have their permissions changed when using the bulk role updater.

cached_entries.title: Hide and restore frames in Workflow Lineage presentations
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now allows you to hide individual frames in presentation mode without deleting them. Hidden frames can be restored later when needed, giving you more flexibility in how you organize and present your workflow visualizations. Navigation buttons automatically disable when all frames in a particular direction are hidden, providing clear visual feedback about available navigation options.

cached_entries.title: Improved handling of auto-save conflicts in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now correctly preserves your latest auto-saved changes when resolving auto-save conflicts. Previously, choosing to abandon changes during an auto-save conflict would revert to the last manually saved state, potentially losing significant work. Now, the system reloads to the most recent auto-saved version, ensuring your latest changes are retained.

cached_entries.title: Improved interface visualization in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now provides a more comprehensive visualization when opening interfaces from Ontology Manager. Instead of displaying object types that implement the interface in a simple grid layout, the system now creates a full dynamic layout showing richer relationships. This enhanced view includes interfaces the chosen interface extends, interfaces that extend it, and object types and interfaces referenced in link type constraints, giving you a more complete picture of your interface relationships.

cached_entries.title: New AIP Analyst Export checkpoint type
cached_entries.description: The **AIP Analyst Export** checkpoint type is now available. Users can now configure checkpoints to appear when exporting AIP Analyst chats to PDF, enabling organizations to capture justifications for these exports.

cached_entries.title: Jump to select component in the layout sidebar
cached_entries.description: Workshop now includes a button in the layout sidebar header that automatically scrolls to and highlights the currently selected widget or layout component. This makes it easier to locate components in the layout tree, especially in complex modules with many elements.

cached_entries.title: Deploy document extraction workflows with AIP Document Intelligence
cached_entries.description: AIP Document Intelligence will be generally available on February 4, 2026 and is enabled by default for all AIP enrollments. AIP Document Intelligence is a low-code application for configuring and deploying document extraction workflows. Users can upload sample documents, experiment with different extraction strategies, and evaluate results based on quality, speed, and cost—all before deploying at scale. AIP Document Intelligence then generates Python transforms that can process entire document collections using the selected strategy, converting PDFs and images into structured Markdown with preserved tables and formatting.

cached_entries.title: GPT-5.2 Codex now available in AIP
cached_entries.description: GPT-5.2 Codex is now available directly from OpenAI for non-georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Improved VS Code workspace and Palantir extension documentation
cached_entries.description: Documentation for VS Code workspaces and the Palantir extension has been reorganized to provide clearer guidance and a more intuitive learning path. The updated documentation makes it easier to find information about specific workflows and features.

cached_entries.title: Transforms documentation improvements
cached_entries.description: Documentation for transforms and VS Code workspaces has been refactored to provide more concise language and better workflow guidance. The updated pages include transforms preview, code-defined filtering, environment debugger, extension features, transforms build, VS Code workspaces overview, and benefits documentation.

cached_entries.title: Multi-ontology support in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage just became a lot more robust - you can now visualize resources across multiple ontologies in one unified graph. Instantly identify cross-ontology relationships, spot external resources at a glance, and switch between ontologies without leaving your workflow view.

cached_entries.title: New Ontology query filter in Resource Management
cached_entries.description: Resource Management now includes an **All Ontology queries** option in the **Source** dropdown of the **Analysis** tab. This new filter allows users to view usage data from Ontology query sources, providing a consolidated view of Ontology-related resource consumption.

cached_entries.title: Fixed date range selection for Today and Yesterday shortcuts
cached_entries.description: The filter list widget and date input components now apply date ranges when using the "Today" or "Yesterday" shortcuts. Previously, these shortcuts included an extra day at the end of the selected range.

cached_entries.title: Introducing Resource Context in Upgrade Assistant
cached_entries.description: Upgrade Assistant now features a Resource Context panel that opens when selecting any resource. The panel displays the resource's current assignees and status alongside a full history of changes including status transitions, assignment updates, ignore actions and initial reporting. Each history entry shows what changed, when and by whom, providing full insight into how a resource reached its current state.

cached_entries.title: OSDK errors displayed in Health Dialog
cached_entries.description: Slate now displays Object Service Development Kit (OSDK) loading errors in the Health Dialog, providing a persistent way to identify and resolve configuration issues. When OSDK fails to load, users can access the new OSDK tab in the Health Dialog to view detailed error information and guidance. The dialog includes a direct link to open the OSDK configuration panel, making it easier to troubleshoot and fix the issue.

cached_entries.title: Improved visibility options for action parameters
cached_entries.description: Workshop now displays visibility options for action parameters without requiring you to select a default value. This makes it easier to configure and view multi-purpose actions where different parameters need to be shown or hidden depending on the context.

cached_entries.title: Improved text wrapping for struct properties in Workshop widgets
cached_entries.description: Workshop now properly supports text wrapping for struct properties and arrays of struct properties in the property list, object table, and object list widgets when the **Enable value wrapping** option is turned on. Additionally, struct values in object tables now show proper ellipsis truncation when content is too long to display fully, with hover tooltips showing the complete value.

cached_entries.title: Improved alignment of variable selector popover
cached_entries.description: The "create new" popover in the variable selector sidebar now aligns precisely with its corresponding menu item, preventing accidental closures when users interact with it.

cached_entries.title: Fixed padding around Gantt chart widget
cached_entries.description: The Gantt charts widget now display without excessive padding, accommodating the recent [Blueprint ↗](https://blueprintjs.com/) design update.

cached_entries.title: View changes and past runs in the new History tab
cached_entries.description: You can now access a complete history of changes and runs for your compute modules in the new **History** tab. This chronological record helps you track configuration changes over time and investigate past deployments for debugging purposes.

cached_entries.title: Automatically package resources in DevOps by tracking a source folder
cached_entries.description: DevOps now supports a new folder tracking mode, allowing products to monitor a specified source folder. Any changes made to the contents of this folder will automatically be detected and incorporated when starting a new version or performing a refresh. Migration helpers are included to help transition existing manually packaged products to the new folder tracking mode.

cached_entries.title: Fixed scrolling in queries panel when hovering over parameter fields
cached_entries.description: Slate now allows scrolling in the queries panel even when the mouse cursor is hovering over parameter input fields. Previously, these input fields would capture scroll events, preventing users from scrolling through the panel while the cursor was positioned over a parameter field.

cached_entries.title: Data source filtering in Queries panel
cached_entries.description: The Queries panel in Slate now includes filtering capabilities that allow application builders to filter queries by data source. When multiple data sources are present, a filter icon appears next to the search input, providing a dropdown menu with checkboxes for each unique data source. Users can select or deselect all data sources using dedicated buttons, and the filter works in conjunction with the existing search functionality.
