cached_entries.title: Improved media display in object list widget
cached_entries.description: Embedded media in the object list widget now displays behind object properties instead of covering them, improving legibility. The background has also been made more opaque to enhance contrast.

cached_entries.title: Improved layout handling for conditionally hidden sections
cached_entries.description: Workshop now properly removes conditionally hidden sections from the layout. Previously, hidden sections would still affect padding and spacing between other sections. With this fix, conditionally hidden sections are completely removed from the layout, ensuring proper alignment and spacing between visible sections.

cached_entries.title: Variable-backed column visibility now works with combined object types in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Workshop now supports variable-backed column visibility filtering in object tables when the **Combine multiple object types** option is enabled. Previously, this filtering functionality only worked when different object types were displayed in separate tabs.

cached_entries.title: Develop and test object views safely with Foundry Branching
cached_entries.description:
Object views are now supported in Foundry Branching, enabling you to develop and test object view changes in isolation before deploying them to production.

Object views are critical interfaces between your ontology and end users. Branching enables safer iteration by allowing you to test changes in isolation, collaborate with multiple teams without conflicts, and ensure quality before deployment—using the same workflows you already use for Workshop modules and Ontology resources.

cached_entries.title: Track function duration and action metrics in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now includes a **Function metrics** tab that provides performance insights for your workflow functions and actions. The **P95 duration** sub-tab displays execution duration at the 95th percentile with intuitive graphs, allowing you to instantly spot performance spikes and proactively address potential issues. The **Executions** sub-tab shows the number of successful and failed executions for functions and actions, helping you identify reliability trends and troubleshoot issues more efficiently. These new metrics provide powerful insights, making it easier to monitor and optimize workflow functions.

cached_entries.title: New run history filtering in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: You can now [filter run history](/aip-observability/run-history/#filter-run-history) in Workflow Lineage to quickly find specific executions of functions, actions, automations, and logic. The **Run history** tab provides a complete view of all executions over the past seven days, and filtering by multiple criteria makes it faster to debug issues or audit workflows in high-volume environments.

cached_entries.title: Automatically package resources in DevOps by tracking a source folder
cached_entries.description: Products packaged in DevOps can now track a source folder. Each time you release a new version, DevOps automatically discovers and includes relevant the resources within the folder. Existing products can be migrated to track a source folder through a one-time migration process. [Learn more about tracking a source folder](https://www.palantir.com/docs/foundry/foundry-devops/folder-tracking/).

cached_entries.title: Run evaluation suites against multiple functions in AIP Evals
cached_entries.description: AIP Evals now supports running evaluation suites against multiple execution targets simultaneously. You can add multiple Foundry functions or Logic functions to an evaluation suite, each with different input/output signatures and evaluator configurations. When configuring a run, you can switch to multi-target mode via the "Test multiple functions" button and select which targets to include. In the results view, you can select and compare results from runs across different targets, allowing you to efficiently evaluate and compare multiple implementations at once. [Learn more about configuring additional target functions in your evaluation suite](/foundry/aip-evals/create-suite/#additional-target-functions).

cached_entries.title: Scrollable derived property previews in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Workshop now displays scrollbars for derived property previews when they contain large values. Previously, these previews could overflow their container, making it difficult to view all content.

cached_entries.title: Monitor usage with rate limit hit tracking in Resource Management
cached_entries.description: The Resource Management application now includes rate limit hit tracking in the **AIP usage and limits** view. Rate limit hits provide visibility into when users, projects, or applications reach their assigned rate limits in Foundry. This feature enables administrators to proactively monitor usage, identify capacity constraints, and troubleshoot issues related to rate limiting.

cached_entries.title: GPT-4.1 mini now available in additional regions
cached_entries.description: GPT-4.1 mini is now available from Azure OpenAI in Australia, Canada, Japan, and the United Kingdom for georestricted enrollments.

cached_entries.title: Approximate nearest neighbor join available in Pipeline Builder
cached_entries.description: Pipeline Builder now offers approximate nearest neighbor (ANN) join functionality for faster semantic search operations. Unlike the KNN join which finds the exact K nearest neighbors for each row, the ANN join uses Spark's ML library to efficiently find estimated close matches. This approach provides substantial improvements in processing speed with only a slight reduction in precision.

cached_entries.title: Active preview for Python transforms
cached_entries.description: Active preview is now available for users of the Palantir extension for Visual Studio Code, enabling automatic updates to Python transform previews every time code is saved. Active preview eliminates the need to manually trigger previews after each code change, so your preview panel maintains synchronization and provides continuous feedback during development. Intelligent caching keeps subsequent previews fast by reusing code-defined filter results, project resources, and dependencies.

cached_entries.title: Quiver nodes now supported in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Users can now view quiver dashboards as nodes in Workflow Lineage graphs. Quiver dashboard nodes function the same as other resource nodes, and have connections to the object types they use and the workshop modules they appear in. Different color modes can be used to view usage metrics, resource permissions, last modified dates, and more.

cached_entries.title: Marketplace parameter variables in Slate
cached_entries.description: Slate now supports configuring variables as Marketplace parameters. Application builders can mark specific variables as parameters that can be customized during Marketplace installation. The Variables Panel includes a visual indicator for variables configured as Marketplace parameters and maintains synchronization when variables are renamed or deleted. Note that global and shared variables are not supported as Marketplace parameters.

cached_entries.title: Improved display of empty strings in array variables
cached_entries.description: Workshop now displays empty strings in array variables as empty quotes (`""`) in the variable preview. Previously, arrays containing empty strings did not clearly indicate the presence of those elements.

cached_entries.title: New checkpoint for opening application now available
cached_entries.description: New checkpoint type now available for `Open AIP Analyst`. You can now use the Checkpoints application to set up a prompt requiring user interaction before AIP Analyst is allowed to load.

cached_entries.title: Extended retention period for function metrics
cached_entries.description: Near real-time metrics for functions are now retained for 30 days instead of 7 days. This extended retention period allows you to observe longer-term performance trends and conduct more comprehensive analysis of your functions over time.

cached_entries.title: View compute usage for builds in the Builds application
cached_entries.description: You can now view compute usage for completed builds in the Builds application. Enable this feature by selecting the settings icon in the application header and toggling **Show compute usage**. When enabled, a new column appears in the builds table displaying the total compute resources consumed by all jobs in a finished build, using the same units shown in the Resource Management application.

cached_entries.title: Temporary media uploads in OSDK and functions
cached_entries.description: Media sets now support temporary media uploads in both OSDK and functions through the new media upload flow. This feature allows you to upload media generated in TSv2 Functions, Python Functions, and improves and simplifies the media upload flow in the OSDK. This new media upload method is side effect free; meaning no more orphaned media items! Temporarily uploaded media remains accessible for one hour before being automatically deleted. This functionality is available in OSDK generator version 2.6.0 or greater. [Learn more about media handling in functions](/foundry/functions/media/).

cached_entries.title: Schedule replica scaling with time-based overrides in Compute Modules
cached_entries.description: You can now schedule automatic scaling adjustments for your compute modules with time-based overrides. On the **Configure** page, set specific minimum and maximum replica counts for designated time periods by specifying days of the week, start and end times, and time zone for when these override settings should automatically apply.

cached_entries.title: Schedule replica scaling with timed overrides in compute modules
cached_entries.description: You can now schedule automatic scaling adjustments for your compute modules with timed overrides. On the **Configure** page, use the new toggle in the scaling section to set specific minimum and maximum replica counts for designated time periods. Specify the days of the week, start and end times, and time zone for when these override settings should automatically apply.

cached_entries.title: Extended retention period for function and action metrics
cached_entries.description: Near real-time metrics for functions and actions are now retained for 30 days instead of 7 days. This extended retention period allows you to observe longer-term performance trends and conduct more comprehensive analysis of your functions and actions over time.

cached_entries.title: Visualize action types with new color mode in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: Workflow Lineage now includes a new Action Rule color mode that visually distinguishes between different types of actions in your workflow. This color-coding system instantly shows whether an action runs a function, creates, modifies, or deletes an object. When used alongside the Action Rule histogram, you can quickly get a comprehensive overview of rule actions across your entire graph and filter actions within your current selection, making it easier to understand and manage complex workflows.

cached_entries.title: New checkpoint type for AIP application access
cached_entries.description: A new checkpoint type is now available for when users open the AIP application. This allows administrators to configure checkpoints that appear when users attempt to access AIP, providing an additional layer of governance for sensitive analytics workflows.

cached_entries.title: Simplified user display in Workshop object tables
cached_entries.description: Workshop object tables now display user information in a simplified format. The new format removes hyperlinks on usernames and no longer shows popular files, while still displaying the user's full name, username, email address, and profile picture.

cached_entries.title: Preserved new lines in metric card tooltip descriptions
cached_entries.description: Metric card widgets now correctly display tooltip descriptions with new lines preserved. Previously, new lines in tooltip descriptions were not rendered, causing formatted text to appear as a single continuous string.

cached_entries.title: Fixed filter pills resizing on hover in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Filter pills in Workshop now maintain a consistent size when hovered over. Previously, these pills would resize on hover, sometimes causing layout shifts and screen flickering at certain widths.

cached_entries.title: Reserved capacity available for LLMs in AIP
cached_entries.description: Reserved capacity is now available by default on many LLMs for non-georestricted enrollments and some US/EU georestricted enrollments. Reserved capacity helps protect critical production workflows from shared project or enrollment limits by reserving dedicated tokens per minute (TPM) and requests per minute (RPM).

cached_entries.title: Extended retention period for function and action metrics
cached_entries.description: Near real-time metrics for functions and actions are now retained for 30 days instead of 7 days. This extended retention period allows you to observe longer-term performance trends and conduct more comprehensive analysis of your functions and actions over time.

cached_entries.title: Updated Automate documentation with improved structure and new content
cached_entries.description: The Automate documentation has been restructured to improve discoverability. Updates include explanations of new features with current interface screenshots, expanded guidance for project and global scope modes, clarified limitations for automation dependencies, and a new workflow for backfilling large object sets using function-backed batches. Access the [full documentation](/foundry/automate/overview/).

cached_entries.title: Configurable markings now available on media reference properties
cached_entries.description: Managing data access and compliance for media sets often requires precise control over how markings are inherited and applied across your ontology. Configurable markings for media reference properties in Ontology Manager are now available on all Foundry enrollments. This capability allows you to define which markings are inherited per media source when configuring media reference properties.

cached_entries.title: Snowflake external pipelines now available
cached_entries.description: [External pipelines](/foundry/building-pipelines/create-external-pipeline-pb/) in Pipeline Builder now support Snowflake. This allows users to orchestrate pipelines in Pipeline Builder while having the execution performed in Snowflake through [snowpark-connect ↗](https://docs.snowflake.com/en/developer-guide/snowpark-connect/snowpark-connect-overview).

cached_entries.title: Developer Console documentation has been reorganized
cached_entries.description: Developer Console documentation has been moved from the Ontology SDK section to its own dedicated section. The content has been reorganized into core concepts and how-to guides page groups for improved navigation. Media assets have been updated and consolidated, and all cross-references and navigation elements have been updated to reflect these changes.

cached_entries.title: Snowflake connector now supports programmatic access token authentication
cached_entries.description: Data Connection now supports programmatic access token (PAT) authentication for Snowflake sources. This authentication method provides an alternative to username and password authentication for connecting to Snowflake data sources. [Learn about authentication in the Snowflake connector](/foundry/available-connectors/snowflake#authentication).

cached_entries.title: Enhanced User Activity Metrics with improved analytics capabilities
cached_entries.description: The User Activity Metrics interface has been redesigned to provide more detailed insights into platform usage. You can now analyze user engagement through multiple dimensions including time periods, application types, engagement types (view/edit), resources, and user groups. This enhanced interface allows administrators to toggle between platform-wide usage metrics and resource-specific analytics for more targeted monitoring and reporting.

cached_entries.title: Tabular data support in Slate Variables panel
cached_entries.description: The **Variables** panel in Slate now supports displaying and editing tabular data in a spreadsheet-like interface. Application builders can view and manipulate data with features such as column resizing, cell editing, and context menus. The panel also supports uploading CSV files with simple tables, making it easier to work with structured data in Slate applications.

cached_entries.title: Fixed model display in Pipeline Builder proposal and diff views
cached_entries.description: Pipeline Builder now correctly displays model information in proposal and diff views. This ensures that imported models are properly identified and displayed across different branches when viewing pipeline changes or during rebase operations.

cached_entries.title: Improved user and viewer group management in Control Panel
cached_entries.description: Control Panel now provides clearer management of user groups and viewer groups. The create/edit form includes a new `User groups` field, and the previously named `Associated groups` field has been renamed to `Viewer groups` for clarity. Additionally, `Initial assignee` and `Viewer group` information now appear as separate badges, with a new `User group` badge added to better distinguish between different types of group assignments.

cached_entries.title: Restrict access to support types in Foundry Issues
cached_entries.description: Platform administrators can now restrict which users are able to apply support types to Foundry issues. This enables the implementation of escalation policies within support setups, such as allowing only L1 support teams to escalate to L2 support teams, while still ensuring reporters can see the new support type applied to their issues.

cached_entries.title: Object Views integrated with Foundry Branching
cached_entries.description: Object Views now work with Foundry Branching, allowing you to add, modify, and remove object views and their associated workshops directly on branches. You can rebase object views from the main branch, deploy changes to object views, and use modified object views from a branch within applications such as Workshop.

cached_entries.title: Observability tab added for object types and link types in Ontology Manager
cached_entries.description: Ontology Manager now includes an Observability tab for object types and link types. This tab displays existing monitor rules applied to the resource or prompts you to set up monitor rules if none exist, making it easier to track and manage monitoring for your ontology resources.

cached_entries.title: Event triggers for string dropdown widget
cached_entries.description: The string dropdown widget in Workshop now supports event triggers on selection and deselection of values. This allows you to create more interactive applications by configuring actions that respond when you select, deselect, or re-select the same value in a dropdown.

cached_entries.title: Auto-fix for YouTube URLs in iframe widget
cached_entries.description: Workshop now detects when a standard YouTube URL is pasted into an iframe widget and offers a one-click solution to convert it to the proper embedded format required for display.

cached_entries.title: Enhanced configuration options for numeric input widgets in Workshop
cached_entries.description: Workshop's numeric input widget now supports additional configuration options. Module builders can set minimum and maximum bounds to restrict input values, define custom step sizes for incremental adjustments, and specify decimal precision up to 10 decimal places. When bounds are set, invalid inputs are automatically clamped to the closest valid value.

cached_entries.title: The VertexAI model family can now be enabled on IL2 enrollments
cached_entries.description: Google Cloud Platform's VertexAI models are now available to be enabled on IL2 enrollments in AIP. VertexAI enabled IL2 enrollments will be able to use Google’s best in class Gemini models and Anthropic Claude 4.5 and Claude 4 model families.

cached_entries.title: Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash now available via VertexAI
cached_entries.description: Gemini 3 series models are now available from VertexAI on commercial, non-georestricted enrollments. Note that Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash are still in preview status from GCP. Within AIP, Gemini 3 Pro and Gemini 3 Flash have all of the characteristics and behavior of a generally available AIP model.

cached_entries.title: Communicate between Foundry instances with the Foundry connector
cached_entries.description: A new connector is now generally available in Data Connection for all enrollments, allowing Foundry instances to interface with each other. Users can now benefit from first class support for communication between Foundry instances, in addition to existing support for communication between Foundry and systems like PostgreSQL, AWS S3, Snowflake, and hundreds more.

cached_entries.title: Configure output write modes for faster pipelines in Pipeline Builder
cached_entries.description: You can now configure different write modes in Pipeline Builder for faster pipelines. Double click on your output node and select **Configure Write mode** to get started. Write modes include: `Snapshot replace`, `Snapshot only new rows`, `Snapshot replace and remove`, `Append only new rows`, and `Always append rows`.

cached_entries.title: Resources can now be pinned to the top of the Files page in Compass
cached_entries.description: Pinned resources display at the top of the Files page of any given project. This allows you to highlight the important files of a project and access them quickly.

cached_entries.title: Manage custom space roles in Control Panel
cached_entries.description: Administrators can now [manage space roles](/foundry/platform-security-management/manage-orgs-and-spaces/#manage-a-space) and workflow permissions from the **Space permissions** page in Control Panel. Each space comes with a set of default roles and the ability to create custom roles for greater flexibility in managing permissions.

cached_entries.title: New ontologies will now use project permissions
cached_entries.description: All ontologies created after Wednesday, January 21, will use the project permissions system by default. With project-based permissions, the ability to view, edit, and manage ontology resources are managed through [Compass](/foundry/compass/overview/), the Palantir platform's filesystem. This is the same permissions system other resource types use.

cached_entries.title: Create test cases from AIP Logic run logs
cached_entries.description: You can now create evaluation test cases directly from historical execution data in AIP Logic. A new **Add as test case** option appears in the Run Log view header, allowing you to quickly convert function execution results into test cases for your evaluation suites. This streamlines the workflow between developing Logic functions and testing them in AIP Evals.

cached_entries.title: Create test cases from execution data in AIP Logic
cached_entries.description: You can now create evaluation test cases directly from historical execution data in AIP Logic. A new **Add as test case** option in the Run Log view allows you to quickly convert execution results into test cases for new or existing evaluation suites. This streamlines the process of building comprehensive test suites based on actual function executions.

cached_entries.title: Filter selected nodes by rule type in Workflow Lineage
cached_entries.description: When selecting multiple nodes in Workflow Lineage, you can now filter by rule type in the details panel. The histogram view includes a new category that groups nodes based on their action rules (create object, run function, etc.), making it easier to identify and select specific types of actions in complex workflows.

cached_entries.title: Connect AI agents to your Ontology using Model Context Protocol (MCP)
cached_entries.description: Ontology MCP enables developers to expose [Developer Console](/foundry/developer-console/overview/) applications to AI agents through the Model Context Protocol (MCP), an open standard for connecting agents to data sources. Resources in your Developer Console application (including objects, actions, and queries) become tools that agents can discover and use. This works with pro-code frameworks (LangChain, CrewAI, custom Python/TypeScript) and low-code platforms (Copilot Studio, other agent builders). Ontology MCP will be available in beta for all users with Developer Console access by the end of January 2026.

cached_entries.title: Lightweight transforms now supports running models as sidecars
cached_entries.description: Available in `palantir_models >=0.2010.0`, lightweight python transforms now supports running [models as sidecar containers](/foundry/integrate-models/transform-model-input/#running-models-as-sidecar-containers). This enables all the benefits of running models as sidecars (stronger Python environment and compute resources separation, simplified usage of models cross-repositories, and more predictable model environments) to the platform's optimized single node compute engine.

cached_entries.title: Set up and inspect monitoring rules for functions and actions in Ontology Manager
cached_entries.description: You can now set up and inspect existing monitoring rules for functions and actions directly in Ontology Manager. This update provides visibility into the active monitoring rule status and history for these resources, making it easier to track and manage their performance.

cached_entries.title: Copy and paste parameter overrides in action forms
cached_entries.description: Ontology Manager now allows you to copy and paste parameter override configurations between parameters in action forms. You can copy overrides from one parameter and paste them to another parameter in the same or different action form. When pasting, known parameter IDs are automatically filled, while unknown parameter IDs prompt you to either map them to available parameters or discard those criteria.

cached_entries.title: Monitor functions and actions with monitoring rules in Ontology Manager
cached_entries.description: Ontology Manager now allows you to set up and inspect monitoring rules directly for functions and actions. You can view active monitoring rule status and history for these resources, providing better visibility into their performance and reliability.

cached_entries.title: Creation of static boolean arrays in variable transformations has been fixed
cached_entries.description: Workshop now allows users to create boolean arrays with static values in variable transformations. Previously, users could only create boolean arrays by referencing existing variable values.

cached_entries.title: Permission check added to Checkpoints scope selection
cached_entries.description: Checkpoints now validates user permissions at the scope selection step. When configuring approval workflows, users must have the appropriate permissions for the selected scope before they can proceed with checkpoint configuration.

cached_entries.title: Fixed Japanese character support in Workshop Excel downloads
cached_entries.description: Workshop now correctly preserves Japanese characters in Excel sheet names when downloading data that includes derived columns.

cached_entries.title: Introducing listeners in Data Connection for capturing inbound webhooks
cached_entries.description: We are excited to introduce listeners [Beta] which enable the receipt of events from external systems that either do not support OAuth 2.0 authentication directly or cannot provide a configurable payload compatible standard Foundry API endpoints. This means you can now receive inbound webhook events in the Palantir platform. The feature is currently in the beta phase of development. To enable on your enrollment, contact Palantir Support. [Learn more from the documentation on Listeners](/foundry/data-connection/listeners-overview/).

cached_entries.title: Approvals required to publish new product versions in Foundry DevOps
cached_entries.description: Marketplace now supports locking down new product versions in a store, requiring approval from a store editor who did not create the product version draft. To approve a new product version, store editors require the `marketplace:finalize-block-set` operation permission on the Marketplace store. [Learn more about managing store permissions in Marketplace](/foundry/foundry-devops/manage-store-permissions/).

cached_entries.title: Cipher Checkpoint UI Cancel Fix
cached_entries.description: [Cipher](/foundry/cipher/overview/) values now remain encrypted after you cancel checkpoints in [Checkpoints](/foundry/checkpoints/overview/).
